entry.title: 4.3 Web Intelligence Data Lineage Challenge
entry.content:
Hi all,

I'm looking for an opinion here.

I have recently been exploring the usability of the new Data view functionality available in BI 4.3 Web Intelligence.

One of the use cases is to build 2 queries from the same universe and join these 2 queries with joins that aren't specified in the source universe. A neat solution to an uncommon problem, but a solution all the same.

So I build a data cube with the data from the two queries with my required joins

The two queries have common objects that are not part of the joins made above and the resulting cube renames the object from the child query with a (1). This looks untidy and when I look at resulting cube, I note that I cannot actually identify the data source within the document dictionary, so I decide to rename my objects via object properties to preserve the data lineage

I was not prepared for what happened next - the renaming of an object in Query 1 renamed the object in Query 2 as well. I have not merged dimensions in this report.

The attachment shows an example of this happening using the efashion universe

I opened a ticket with Support and have been told that this is working as expected, but I don't think this makes sense

Does anyone else have an opinion on this?

regards

James

entry.title: Re: atc error-invalid name ENQUEUE_PROCESSED for DATA/STA...
entry.content:
As already said, the variable name doesn't fit the naming conventions defined in your company.

You forgot to say that the function module was generated by SAP (function modules starting with VIEWFRAME are generated by the Table Maintenance Generator), i.e. it was not written by you.

ATC should not run neither on SAP source code nor on source code generated by SAP. Ask your team to configure ATC to omit all these units of SAP source code.

entry.title: Re: SAP BPA - Environment Variables
entry.content:
Environmental variable will not work for you. The IDP / IAS based solution, proposed by Dan will work, but each change will require IDP or IAS admin privileges. Another solution, where approvers could be managed by designated person without any IDP / IAS admin privileges would be as follows:

1. get a database like HANA Cloud or any other, where you can expose the table vis ODATA and create the approvers table.

2. Build approvers administration app in Build Apps, which uses the approvers table exposed by ODATA and give approvers administrator access to this app.

3. Let approvers administrator put approvers in the table.

4. Create ACTION in SBPA to get data by ODATA from approvers table.

5. Use process variable to store the approver fetched by ACTION for the process.

6. Use this process variable in the "Approvers" field of your approval form.

This solution much more complicated than the one proposed by Dan, but it does not require IDP / IAS admin privileges to maintain approvers.

entry.title: Re: How to create custom Cloud foundry Tasks in SAP BTP?
entry.content:
Hello Thomas,

I think you already found the most important sources of information. SAP BTP CF is based on the open source CF distribution that is why the sources you found should apply. 

To try a task execution I just deployed the cf-sample-app-nodejs application to SAP BTP with the name cf-nodejs-bv-test. After the deployment I could execute a task on it with:

cf run-task cf-nodejs-bv-test --command "echo hello" --name example-task

In the application logs the output of the task could be found like:

cf logs cf-nodejs-bv-test --recent
...
   2024-11-11T12:16:39.94+0200 [APP/TASK/example-task/0] OUT hello
...

Additional sources to check are the CF stack docs especially the docs about the cflinuxfs4 receipt which describes what is included in the stack (the root file system of the container) which is currently offered on SAP BTP CF and that is why the echo command from above works . I also found this Stack Overflow discussion interesting which describes e.g. how to execute a Java command with the Java buildpack. I hope this helps further.

entry.title: HOD/HR Able to Edit Job Information via Action Button Before Selecting Event/Reason
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have a specific requirement from one of our customers.

Currently, HODs and HR are able to modify job information from the Action Button (top left corner) before selecting the event and event reasons. However, once the event and event reasons are chosen, the previously filled fields are being saved.

Issue Details:

  • HODs and HR can update any job information fields via the Action Button before specifying the event and event reasons.
  • After selecting the event and event reasons, the system is saving the changes made earlier, even though this should not occur until the event is confirmed.
  • Our rule specifies that fields should only become visible after the correct event reason is selected.
  • Currently, users can make changes to any fields before selecting the event reason, and these changes are being saved irrespective of the event reason chosen.

Expected Behavior:

  • Job information should only be saved and finalized once the event and event reasons are properly selected and confirmed.
  • Fields should become visible and editable only after the relevant event reason is selected.
  • Changes should be saved only after the correct event reason has been specified and confirmed.

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Use setAccountResidency before calling accounts.regis...
entry.content:
Hi,

 

The WebSDK is just an interface for the APIs. This means you can use that parameter for 'accounts.register' whether or not the request is coming from an SDK or directly to the API.

Please refer to the following Product Documentation about Global Registration Flow.

 

Best Regards,

SAP Solution Support

entry.title: Re: HOD/HR Able to Edit Job Information via Action Button...
entry.content:
@SatishPanda wrote:

Hi Experts,

We have a specific requirement from one of our customers.

Currently, HODs and HR are able to modify job information from the Action Button (top left corner) before selecting the event and event reasons. However, once the event and event reasons are chosen, the previously filled fields are being saved.

Issue Details:

  • HODs and HR can update any job information fields via the Action Button before specifying the event and event reasons.
  • After selecting the event and event reasons, the system is saving the changes made earlier, even though this should not occur until the event is confirmed.
  • Our rule specifies that fields should only become visible after the correct event reason is selected.
  • Currently, users can make changes to any fields before selecting the event reason, and these changes are being saved irrespective of the event reason chosen.

Expected Behavior:

  • Job information should only be saved and finalized once the event and event reasons are properly selected and confirmed.
  • Fields should become visible and editable only after the relevant event reason is selected.
  • Changes should be saved only after the correct event reason has been specified and confirmed.

Thank you.

Hello,
To address your issue: mypoints search

Current Problem:

  • HODs and HR can modify job information before selecting event reasons, and these changes are being saved immediately.

Expected Behavior:

  • Job information should only be saved after event and event reasons are confirmed.

  • Fields should be visible and editable only after selecting the event reason.

Solution:

  • Ensure job information changes are saved only upon confirmation of the event reason.

  • Adjust the system to make fields visible and editable only after the event reason is selected.

    Best Regards,
    James Keen

entry.title: Join Our Espresso Session: Unlocking the Potential of GenAI in the Utilities Industry on November 19
entry.content:
Dear Utilities Community,

The utility industry is currently undergoing a significant transformation through the use of artificial intelligence. 

AI will have a massive impact on the business processes of utility companies, enabling unprecedented efficiency gains in processes and offering users of IT systems an unprecedented user experience. The consistent decentralization of energy generation, which is of central importance for a sustainable energy transition, will hardly be possible without AI-supported technologies. GenAI will also fundamentally change the way energy suppliers interact with their customers, revolutionizing customer service.

We are delighted to announce our upcoming webcast "Unlocking the Potential of GenAI in the Utilities Industry" on Tuesday, November 19 from 4pm - 4:30pm CET. 

During this session, you will:

  • Gain insights into the strategic priorities related to AI in the utilities industry
  • Explore AI-powered scenarios and use cases across various business functions
  • Learn about SAP solutions with AI capabilities

 

Speakers: 

Stefan Engelhardt: VP Industry Business Unit Utilities at SAP

Bruno Pincolini: Business Development for Utilities at SAP

Please join us next week and register here 

See you there!

entry.title: Re: Open Hub table in SE11 is empty
entry.content:
Hi David

If the openhub is defined to write data to a remote database (via DBCON), then the local OpenHub Table is empty as the data is directly written to the remote database.

 

br

Roland

entry.title: DevOps with SAP BTP hands-on videos now on SAP Learning
entry.content:
In 2021, our colleague @PhilipMugglestone had created a series of hands-on tutorial videos that helped many learners to set up SAP BTP to apply DevOps principles for application development (announcement blog post from that time). As the videos provide a holistic end-to-end view on how to set up the involved SAP BTP services in detail, we still see value in providing those videos, even if some aspects might be outdated. As a result, we now migrated them to SAP Learning.

Please note that the videos were originally published on the SAP HANA Academy YouTube Channel and therefore may contain references to SAP HANA Academy and YouTube that are no longer valid. Also, updates and additional links are provided in the description of several of the videos, so please use them with caution and make sure to check out their full description, as marked in below's screenshot of an example video:

Example of additional information provided in video descriptions

Here the links to the videos on SAP Learning, in the right order:

Title and LinkDescription
IntroductionIntroduction to DevOps with SAP BTP, incl. SAP BTP service entitlements used during the scenario
Getting StartedGet an idea on how to configure a developer landscape in SAP BTP
Develop ApplicationUsage of SAP Business Application Studio, cloning of a Git repository
SAP Continuous Integration and Delivery serviceLearn how to configure and use SAP BTP's Pipeline-as-a-Service offering
SAP Cloud Transport Management Pt 1Here, transport management is getting set up, incl. subscription and creation of SAP BTP subaccounts
SAP Cloud Transport Management Pt 2Creation of destinations to source and target nodes, setup of transport landscape, interplay of pipeline with transport management
SAP Cloud Transport Management Pt 3Handling of the transport along the transport landscape
SAP Alert Notification service for SAP BTPLearn how to use SAP Alert Notification service to automatically send a message to a selected Slack channel whenever transport management starts or finishes an import
SAP Automation Pilot Pt 1Learn how to set up a smoke test using SAP Automation Pilot
SAP Automation Pilot Pt 2Part two of setting up the smoke test that gets executed automatically whenever transport management finishes an import, incl. sending of a status message to your preferred Slack channel

General Ledger 0L - Fiscal year variant (K4) for IFRS

General Ledger 1L - Fiscal Year Variant(V3) for Local Tax

General Ledger 2L - Fiscal Year Variant(K4)  for Asset Local Tax

entry.title: Re: Configuration Transport Center - Preparing Transport Request from Source tenant
entry.content:
Hello Avinash,

Currently, this is not supported. We suggest you raise an enhancement request on the influence portal for the same.

entry.title: SAP ILM : Blocking a partner does not work
entry.content:
Hello team, 

I am trying to install the SAP ILM solution to see if it meets my needs regarding the archiving and deletion of BPs (business partners).

I have currently activated the services in SICF and the business functions in SFW5.

SICF 

 

SFW5

 



I have created residence and retention rules using IRM_POL for ILM object CA_BUPA, FI_ACCRECV, FI_ACCKVNK and FI_PAYB for audit area BUPA_DP.

Example 

I’m not including screenshots of the other rules, but I can provide them if needed.

In the back-end system, I run the BUPA_PREPARE_EOP program, and it does indicate that the BP can be blocked, but it doesn’t actually do it.

I don’t understand how to add this customization point, system group, for the program to work.

Do you have any ideas or have you done this kind of process before?

Thank you in advance.

PS: I’m on a sandbox environment, so maybe that’s why some settings are missing for the BPs

entry.title: Re: Enterprise Management Layer for SAP S/4HANA – What’s New – Release 2023
entry.content:
Hi Satinder,

Sorry for the late reply !

EML is a service that needs to be acquired through SAP Services. It can be deployed in the context of RiSE, but is a separate service. We provide a database backup with the EML content. Activating SAP Best Practices can take longer if many countries need to be implemented.

I hope I could answer your questions.

Best regards, Petra Ohlinger

entry.title: Re: Authorization to create CDS views
entry.content:
The permissions you mentioned are generally right for creating CDS views in SAP HANA.

May you need additional ones like S_DEVELOP for ABAP development, S_CDS for CDS operations, S_RFC for executing remote function calls, S_TABU_DIS for displaying table contents, S_TRANSPRT for transporting objects, and S_USER_GRP for user group maintenance.

These depend on your specific setup and needs.

This works fine but the GL account requires a cost center to be posted on. 

Would someone know if there is a way to spread the unplanned delivery cost among the cost centers of the invoice items?

Eli

entry.title: Re: The new SAP Digital Manufacturing for Insights, including MDOs, Operational Reports and Embedded
entry.content:
@ganesh_vc , there is definitely no retention setup available, as there is currently no data retention period or limit. Perhaps your system really does not have data older than 6 months? Or, perhaps you have issue with your filters? In any case, I suggest you raise a ticket for further clarification.

I'm not sure what you mean by "MDO data for eSAC in DM are not showing up in the integration message dashboard", as Integration Message Dashboard (IMD) shows basically ERP integrations and indeed does not show MDO or eSAC data.

Hope it helps,
Manoel

entry.title: Re: HANA SQL to give a BPC user name and get the dimension members that they can access
entry.content: There is no direct way to get this information using HANA SQL because the authorization data is stored in BW authorization objects and not in HANA tables.

entry.title: ASUG Tech Connect 2024 | Keynote Highlights & Resources
entry.content:
Today, the Americas’ SAP Users’ Group (ASUG) hosted the  first SAP TechEd on Tour stop in Florida! Together with Geoff Scott, CEO of ASUG, and Patrick Dineen, SVP Finance Transformation Systems at NBC Universal, we explored how SAP is helping organizations accelerate ERP modernization and drive innovation across industries with SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP), SAP Joule, SAP Build, and SAP Integration Suite. 

In this blog post, I've compiled all relevant links and demos presented during my segment of the keynote – and more!  

If you want to learn more about SAP TechEd on Tour and other stops, go to https://www.sap.com/events/teched.html.  

 

Best of TechEd – Virtual keynote recap  

The main SAP Business Technology Platform announcements from our virtual keynote focused on three key points: 

  • First: We help customers get faster, better, and automated: SAP BTP serves all their needs to innovate, integrate, and extend their systems.  
  • Second: We are enhancing the developer and user experience with our latest release of SAP Build, which now includes ABAP Cloud.   
  • And third: We are raising the bar for Business AI – for a whole new level of productivity.  

You will find all announcements shared at SAP TechEd 2024 in our News Guide and the replay of our opening keynote. 

 

Best of Platform 

SAP BTP is your platform of choice for application development, automation, integration, data & analytics, and Business AI. 

To support your journey with BTP, we provide a wide range of tools, best practices, resources, and reference architectures designed to help you:

  • Design and deploy enterprise-grade solutions,
  • accelerate time-to-value and optimize costs, and
  • foster collaboration and continuous learning in your architectural practices.

How to get started? At ASUG TechConnect, I highlighted some of the key tools and resources available to get you started effectively:

The SAP BTP Guidance Framework helps you design solutions that align with your company’s digital strategy. You can find multiple BTP reference architectures already at the SAP Discovery Center with the possibility to adopt it to your requirements by using the BTP solution diagram repository.

You can find more information here on SAP Help. Or simply tune into our SAP TechEd session on demand:

  • Start your journey with SAP BTP and confidently accelerate adoption

The SAP Discovery Center provides you with hands-on guidance to implement BTP services  based on real use cases! You can activate them in your BTP landscape with the new quick account setup. With a single click you will get all services provisioned and deployed. Learn more about this in the respective SAP TechEd on-demand sessions:

  • Get started with SAP BTP: Discover and accelerate with SAP Discovery Center
  • Modernize your technology landscape with SAP BTP and SAP LeanIX solutions

The SAP Business Accelerator Hub equips you with additional building blocks to realize your digital strategy. More than 25,000 pre-built assets are already available, such as Integration flows, APIs, Business Processes, adapters, validated partner use cases and a lot more.

The SAP BTP GenAI starter kit empowers you to integrate AI capabilities into your infrastructure easily. Get a first overview in this blog post and more detailed information here on GitHub.

Once you’re set up with BTP, a world of possibilities opens up. In our keynote, I highlighted why BTP stands as the premier platform for embedding AI, accelerating developer productivity, building applications, and seamlessly integrating SAP with third-party solutions:

AI Foundation on BTP
The AI Foundation on BTP helps you make the most out of your SAP data and processes. You also get the broadest selection of foundational models from top providers—all in one place. Find out more through our SAP TechEd on-demand sessions:

  • Development with our generative AI hub, SDKs, and orchestration features
  • Enable more secure and responsible generative AI

Joule and Joule studio
By year-end, our AI copilot Joule will cover 80% of SAP's top workflows, enabling users to manage timesheets, view returns, create reports, and compare quotes through natural language. Integrated with SAP Build, it also boosts developer productivity. Discover additional insights in the following SAP TechEd on-demand session:

  • Extend Joule with SAP Build and develop custom enterprise skills

Joule Agents
Joule is going agentic! Powered by large language models, AI agents tackle complex business problems through planning, self-reflection, reasoning, and collaboration.

SAP Build x ABAP
Two years ago, we launched SAP Build as a unified low-code environment. Last year, we added SAP Build Code for AI-driven app development. Now, SAP Build also integrates directly with SAP S/4HANA, enabling BAPI and function module calls in the S/4HANA Private Cloud. And we now also included ABAP Cloud in our latest release of SAP Build!

We also introduced the new ABAP explain agent, allowing users to quickly understand complex ABAP code by generating explanations through large language models. Learn more in our TechEd on-demand sessions: 

  • Enhance your fusion team collaboration with ABAP Cloud and SAP Build
  • Boost your coding efficiency: Explore Joule's ABAP developer capabilities

Integration Suite
With SAP Integration Suite, we offer you a modern solution to run your integration scenarios across SAP and third-party applications. You benefit from embedded AI, hundreds of connectors, and thousands of pre-built integrations. Already 75% of our customers use SAP Integration Suite to integrate with non-SAP systems. And we are introducing new integration adapters for Anaplan, Coupa, HubSpot, NetSuite ERP, Snowflake, and more.

  • Innovate now with a modern, AI-assisted integration suite

 

Best of Suite

With SAP BTP powering our suite of enterprise applications, we also explored how everything connects across our portfolio. A suite-first approach ensures a seamless, productive user experience throughout the workday.

As an example, we presented a demo that shows how the end users of our products can easily navigate across our solution portfolio via SAP Start. Infused by our copilot Joule, users can be more productive when setting up new teams, booking travel, or getting insights through SAP Analytics Cloud, for example. You can watch the full demo here.

  •  View our Integration Strategy Paper

 

Get started now!

To get started with BTP and Business AI, here are some of our missions in the SAP Discovery Center:

  • AI on BTP: https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/missiondetail/4338/4621/
  • GenAI with SAP Build Code: https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/missiondetail/4441/4727/
  • Gen AI with RAG for CAP: https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/missiondetail/4371/4655/ 

Here are some additional missions to kick off your journey with BTP: 

  • App Development on BTP: https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/missiondetail/4431/4717/
  • Integration Suite: https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/missiondetail/3258/3327/
  • Best Of Suite: https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/missiondetail/4451/4737/

I hope you are all fired up now and ready to get your hands dirty.

We can’t wait to see what you will build!

entry.title: CL_GUI_HTML_VIEWER does not work when called in flowers app
entry.content:
Hi all,
I created a report that shows attachments with cl_gui_html_viewer.
If I run the report from gui it works. If I call the report via Tcode from Fiori launchpad it doesn't display anything

I attach code

 

Thanks

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Report ZVIEW_ALLEGATI
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*&
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
REPORT zview_allegati.

DATA: okcode              TYPE sy-ucomm,
      va_nomefile         TYPE string,
      ca_initial_dir      TYPE string,
      g_custom_container3 TYPE REF TO cl_gui_custom_container,
      o_viewer            TYPE REF TO cl_gui_html_viewer,
      va_curr_url         TYPE url.

START-OF-SELECTION.
  IF 1 = 2. ENDIF.

  CALL SCREEN 2050.

END-OF-SELECTION.
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Module STATUS_2050 OUTPUT
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*&
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
MODULE status_2050 OUTPUT.
  SET PF-STATUS 'ZSTANDARD'.
  SET TITLEBAR 'ZSTANDARD'.

  IF NOT va_nomefile IS INITIAL.
    PERFORM create_object2 .
  ENDIF.

  IF va_nomefile IS INITIAL.
    PERFORM refresh_object.
  ENDIF.

ENDMODULE.
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*&      Module  USER_COMMAND_2050  INPUT
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*       text
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
MODULE user_command_2050 INPUT.
  CASE okcode.
    WHEN '&F03' OR '&F15' OR '&F12'.
      PERFORM exit_program.
    WHEN 'BROWSE'.
      PERFORM browse_for_file USING TEXT-001 CHANGING va_nomefile.
    WHEN 'ERASE'.
      CLEAR va_nomefile.
  ENDCASE.

ENDMODULE.
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Form exit_program
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& text
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& -->  p1        text
*& <--  p2        text
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
FORM exit_program .

  IF NOT o_viewer IS INITIAL.
    CALL METHOD o_viewer->close_document
      EXCEPTIONS
        cntl_error = 1
        OTHERS     = 2.
    CALL METHOD o_viewer->free
      EXCEPTIONS
        cntl_error        = 1
        cntl_system_error = 2
        OTHERS            = 3.
  ENDIF.

  LEAVE TO SCREEN 0.
ENDFORM.
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Form browse_for_file
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& text
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*&      --> TEXT_001
*&      <-- VA_NOMEFILE
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
FORM browse_for_file  USING   p_text
                      CHANGING p_nomefile.

  DATA: tb_l_files TYPE filetable,
        vn_l_rc    TYPE i.

  CALL METHOD cl_gui_frontend_services=>file_open_dialog
    EXPORTING
      window_title            = p_text
*     default_extension       =
*     default_filename        =
*     file_filter             =
*     with_encoding           =
      initial_directory       = ca_initial_dir
      multiselection          = ' '
    CHANGING
      file_table              = tb_l_files
      rc                      = vn_l_rc
*     user_action             =
*     file_encoding           =
    EXCEPTIONS
      file_open_dialog_failed = 1
      cntl_error              = 2
      error_no_gui            = 3
      not_supported_by_gui    = 4
      OTHERS                  = 5.

  IF sy-subrc <> 0.
* MESSAGE ID SY-MSGID TYPE SY-MSGTY NUMBER SY-MSGNO
*            WITH SY-MSGV1 SY-MSGV2 SY-MSGV3 SY-MSGV4.
  ENDIF.

  READ TABLE tb_l_files INDEX 1 ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<r_l_files>).
  IF sy-subrc = 0 AND <r_l_files> IS ASSIGNED.
    p_nomefile = <r_l_files>.
  ENDIF.
  CLEAR: sy-subrc.
  UNASSIGN: <r_l_files>.


ENDFORM.
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Form create_object2
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& text
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& -->  p1        text
*& <--  p2        text
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
FORM create_object2 .

  IF NOT va_nomefile IS INITIAL.
    IF NOT g_custom_container3 IS INITIAL.
      PERFORM refresh_object.
    ENDIF.

    CREATE OBJECT g_custom_container3
      EXPORTING
        container_name              = 'CONTAINER2'
*  ADD
      EXCEPTIONS
        cntl_error                  = 1
        cntl_system_error           = 2
        create_error                = 3
        lifetime_error              = 4
        lifetime_dynpro_dynpro_link = 5
        OTHERS                      = 6.

    CREATE OBJECT o_viewer
      EXPORTING
        parent             = g_custom_container3
*       saphtmlp           = 'X' ADD
      EXCEPTIONS
        cntl_error         = 1
        cntl_install_error = 2
        dp_install_error   = 3
        dp_error           = 4
        OTHERS             = 5.

  ENDIF.

  IF NOT g_custom_container3 IS INITIAL.

*  ADD
    CALL METHOD o_viewer->enable_simplebrowsing
      EXPORTING
        benable    = 1
      EXCEPTIONS
        cntl_error = 1
        others     = 2
            .
    IF sy-subrc <> 0.
*     Implement suitable error handling here
    ENDIF.

    va_curr_url = va_nomefile.
    CALL METHOD o_viewer->show_url
      EXPORTING
        url                    = va_curr_url
*       frame                  =
*       in_place               = 'X'
      EXCEPTIONS
        cntl_error             = 1
        cnht_error_not_allowed = 2
        cnht_error_parameter   = 3
        dp_error_general       = 4
        OTHERS                 = 5.
  ENDIF.
*  ENDIF.
ENDFORM.
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Form refresh_object
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& text
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& -->  p1        text
*& <--  p2        text
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
FORM refresh_object .
  IF NOT g_custom_container3 IS INITIAL.
    CALL METHOD g_custom_container3->free
      EXCEPTIONS
        cntl_error        = 1
        cntl_system_error = 2
        OTHERS            = 3.

    CALL METHOD o_viewer->free
      EXCEPTIONS
        cntl_error        = 1
        cntl_system_error = 2
        OTHERS            = 3.
  ENDIF.
ENDFORM.

END
WHEN ISNUMERIC(T2.FormID) = 0 THEN
T2.FormID
END
AS 'Form Description',                        
T2.ItemID as 'Area/header field where FMS is assigned',                        
T2.ColID as 'Column field where FMS is assigned',
T2.FieldID as 'Auto refresh Field'                          
FROM   OQCN T0                           
INNER JOIN OUQR T1                
ON T0.CategoryId = T1.QCategory 
INNER JOIN CSHS T2                
ON T1.IntrnalKey = T2.QueryId   
WHERE  T0.CategoryId != -2
